MOTION - Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Punitive Damages
Off Calendar Aug-13-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT RAYMOND SCOTT'S Motion To Strike Punitive Damages is granted with leave to amend. Plaintiff's complaint for negligence does not allege specific facts that would give rise to punitive damages. (See, e.g., Hilliard v. A.H. Robins Co. (1983) 148 Cal.App.3d 390-921.) Many of the allegations set out in plaintiff's opposition are not asserted in his complaint.

Prevailing/Moving party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-25-2021 Continued To Aug-25-21 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-18-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT RAYMOND SCOTT'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT:

Raymond Scott's "motion to strike complaint" is ordered off calendar for noncompliance with CCP 435.5. That law requires not only a "meet and confer in person or by telephone" before a motion to strike may be filed (id.at (a)), but also a declaration stating the "means by which the moving party met and conferred" (id. at (a)(3)(A)). The parties did not comply with the code. The parties are ordered to meet and confer immediately and Scott's response to the complaint is now due by July 9, 2021.

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
